meta.reportLabel: Wed Report — ESCALATION: 3 ships hit in Hormuz, US strikes Iran, oil sanctions reimposed - WTI ~$70-72 (+5% after hours), war premium rebuilding; RBNZ delivered the DOVISH hike to 2.50% (higher hurdle for the next); both high-conviction CAD shorts challenged - board reset, no high-conviction pairs today; Fed June minutes 18:00 UTC.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: Regime transition: the ceasefire cracked overnight (3 merchant ships hit in Hormuz, CENTCOM strikes on Iranian anti-ship sites, oil sanctions reimposed) and the war premium is being rebuilt - WTI ~$70-72, Brent ~$76. That rescues CAD (both consensus CAD shorts challenged), hands USD and JPY competing haven bids, and hits AUD/NZD/GER40. RBNZ hiked to 2.50% but scripted it dovish. Everything is headline-hostage until the Doha talks question (Sat Jul 11) resolves.
meta.nextBigEvent: Wed Jul 8, 18:00 UTC: Fed June minutes; then the Doha-talks convening question (Sat Jul 11)

dailyRead: The board reset overnight: two tankers (Qatari LNG carrier Al Rekayat, Saudi supertanker Wedyan) were hit in the Strait of Hormuz Tuesday, the IRGC struck a third vessel within 24 hours, Washington reimposed the oil sanctions lifted under the June 17 MoU, and CENTCOM launched 'powerful strikes' on Iranian air defenses, radar and anti-ship sites early Wednesday - WTI settled +2.8% at ~$70.4 and jumped another ~5% after hours toward ~$72 (Brent ~$76). The war premium the market spent three weeks crushing is back, which rescues CAD (both former high-conviction CAD shorts are directly challenged), bids USD and JPY as competing havens, and hits AUD/NZD/GER40. Meanwhile the RBNZ delivered its widely expected but DOVISH 25bp hike to 2.50% (first in three years; 4 of 6 members see risks balanced; a 'considerably higher hurdle' for the next move) - NZD popped and faded. Cleanest expressions now run through JPY strength (short USD/JPY, AUD/JPY; long JPYBASKET) and long USOIL/XAU while the strikes are live; Fed June minutes land tonight 18:00 UTC.

today.headline: Oil re-spike rescues the board's weakest currency — WTI ~$70-72 (+5% AH)
today.read: The glut thesis met a tanker war: the floor under CAD firms and both consensus CAD shorts (USD/CAD, AUD/CAD longs) are challenged - do not fade the squeeze early. Friday's June jobs and BoC Jul 15 are next; de-escalation re-pins it.

today.headline: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% — but scripted it dovish; NZD popped and faded
today.read: 6-0 on the hike, 4 of 6 see risks balanced, and the market read a 'considerably higher hurdle' for the next move - Sept likely but not aggressively priced. NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) validates or breaks the framing; NZD is post-event rangebound vs USD.

today.headline: Haven bid finally arrives — and it points the SAME way as the MoF asymmetry
today.read: Risk-off JPY demand at ~162, deep in the proven intervention zone (Y11.73tn precedent), gives JPY its first genuine catalyst in weeks: USD/JPY and AUD/JPY shorts upgraded, JPYBASKET long upgraded. De-escalation is the reversal risk.

today.headline: Haven bid + hawkish-hold Fed; June minutes tonight 18:00 UTC
today.read: The oil re-spike re-arms the energy-CPI floor and keeps the Sept hike (~50%) alive; minutes are stale by construction (pre-NFP, pre-escalation) - the Jul 14 CPI is still the arbiter. DXY holds >101 with a long tilt.

today.headline: Risk-off beta hit — the escalation finds the thinnest hike tail first
today.read: Aug 11 was already only ~36% priced; an oil-shock growth scare argues patience, so AUD wears the beta (AUD/JPY, AUD/USD shorts). The Q2 CPI in late July is the re-arm date; a de-escalation snap-back is the squeeze risk.

today.headline: No change — soft with the core wrinkle; oil complicates the dovish read
today.read: Flash 2.8% cooling still guts Jul 23 urgency, but core 2.5% plus a fresh energy shock keeps the Sept hike alive - EUR/USD short works on the USD leg. The squeeze risk is a hawkish Jul 23 tone.

today.headline: No change — re-armed BoE thesis holds; oil adds to the hawks' case
today.read: Services CPI 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30, and the energy re-spike strengthens their hand. Firm, rangebound vs USD; long tilt vs AUD.

strength.tag: oil re-spike re-arms the CPI floor; minutes tonight
strength.drivers: CPI 4.2% headline / core 2.9% (May); June NFP +57k big miss (unemp 4.2% on a participation drop to 61.5%); Fed 3.50-3.75% hawkish hold, zero 2026 cuts priced, Sept hike ~50%; fresh haven bid on the Iran escalation.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hawkish hold into the Jul 28-29 FOMC with a fresh haven bid; the oil re-spike re-arms the energy-CPI floor and keeps the Sept tail alive. Tonight's minutes are stale by construction (pre-NFP, pre-escalation); the Jun CPI (Jul 14) is the arbiter. Risk: a second soft CPI print turns the NFP crack into a trend. Next FOMC Jul 28-29.

strength.tag: services CPI 3.7%; oil adds to the hawks' case
strength.drivers: Core 2.6% (May, up from 2.5%); services CPI 3.7% (up from 3.2%); headline 2.8% held; DMP wages 4.2%. BoE 3.75%, hawkish 7-2 hold (2 hike votes); quiet tape, thesis unchanged.
strength.forward: 🦅 Hawkish - the services re-acceleration keeps the hike tail and both dissents alive into the Jul 30 MPR round, and the new oil spike adds to the energy-CPI floor. Risk: a soft activity/labor wave re-splits the MPC. Next BoE Jul 30.

strength.tag: 2.50%, first hike since 2023; higher hurdle next
strength.drivers: RBNZ hiked 25bp to 2.50% today, 6-0 - but the statement was the dovish version: headline inflation now seen PEAKED at 3.9% in Q2 (easing to 3.3% Q3), 4 of 6 members (incl. Gov. Breman) call risks balanced vs 2 upside. NZD popped and faded; market reads a considerably higher hurdle for the next hike.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Hike delivered but guidance capped it - Sept still likely, not aggressively priced; Aug 19 should be a hold. NZ Q2 CPI (~Jul 16) validates or breaks the 'peaked at 3.9%' framing. Risk: a 4%+ CPI print re-arms the hawks; risk-off beta cuts the other way. Next RBNZ Aug 19.

strength.tag: haven bid + MoF asymmetry now aligned
strength.drivers: Core CPI 1.4% (May, subsidy-suppressed); unemp 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26 tight. BoJ 1.00% (hiked Jun 16, 7-1), guides more hikes - the oil re-spike feeds its inflation case. Spot ~162 was deep in the proven MoF zone (Y11.73tn/$74bn precedent) BEFORE the strikes; risk-off now pulls the same direction.
strength.forward: 🦅 The escalation hands JPY the haven bid on top of the intervention asymmetry - the first genuine catalyst in weeks; BoJ Jul 31 stays hawkish. Risk: a rapid de-escalation restores the carry grind; for longs the ~250bp carry bleed resumes if the tape calms. Next BoJ Jul 31.

strength.tag: thin Aug tail (~36%) meets an oil-shock scare
strength.drivers: May CPI: trimmed mean 3.6% (up from 3.4%); headline 4.0%; unemp 4.5% loosening (June jobs Jul 16). RBA 4.35% (held Jun after 75bp of 2026 hikes); the Aug 11 hike was already priced only ~36% and called an outlier before the escalation.
strength.forward: ⚖️🔻 The sticky-core floor stands but risk-off hits the beta first - an oil-shock growth scare argues RBA patience through Q3. Risk: fast de-escalation plus a hot Q2 CPI (late Jul) flips it straight back to hawkish. Next RBA Aug 11.

strength.tag: flash 2.8% cooling BUT core 2.5%; oil complicates
strength.drivers: EZ June flash HICP 2.8% (down from 3.2%) BUT core ROSE to 2.5% from 2.2%. Unemp 6.3%. ECB 2.25% depo (hiked Jun 11, first since 2023), meeting-by-meeting; the fresh energy shock stalls the 'headline keeps cooling' leg of the dovish read.
strength.forward: ⚖️ Cooling headline guts Jul 23 urgency (hold expected) but rising core plus the oil re-spike keep a Sept hike alive - the dovish EUR read is not clean. Risk: a hawkish Jul 23 tone on core + energy squeezes EUR shorts. Next ECB Jul 23.

strength.tag: glut thesis met a tanker war; WTI ~$70-72
strength.drivers: May CPI 3.2% (energy-driven; ex-energy 2.1%, trim ~2.0%); unemp 6.6%; May +88k (June jobs Friday); wages 3.0% cooling; technical recession. BoC 2.25%, 5th straight hold. WTI jumped to ~$70-72 (+5% after hours) on the Hormuz escalation - the floor under the board's weakest currency firms.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Still the softest macro on the board but the oil bid firms the floor; BoC sidelined Jul 15 (core ~2% vs energy headline); Friday's June jobs is the domestic catalyst. Risk: de-escalation re-opens the glut and re-pins CAD; a soft jobs print does the same. Next BoC Jul 15.

3 vessels hit, CENTCOM strikes ongoing, sanctions reimposed; every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY, gold.

geopolitics: The ceasefire cracked. Tuesday two tankers - the Qatari-owned LNG carrier Al Rekayat (engine-room fire) and the Saudi-flagged supertanker Wedyan - were struck by projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz, and within 24 hours the IRGC hit a third merchant vessel. Washington's response came in two waves: Treasury reimposed the Iran oil-export sanctions lifted under the June 17 Islamabad Memorandum, then CENTCOM launched a 'series of powerful strikes' early Wednesday on Iranian air defenses, radar sites, anti-ship missile batteries and dozens of IRGC small boats near the strait. Oil repriced immediately: WTI settled +2.8% at ~$70.4 and jumped another ~5% after hours toward ~$72; Brent popped to ~$76 - the war premium the market crushed over three weeks of glut headlines (Iranian crude stacking at sea, Saudi at 90% of baseline, record Russian exports) is being rebuilt. The timing compounds it: Khamenei's funeral rites run through Thursday, the Doha talks scheduled for Saturday are now openly in doubt, and the ~Aug 16 expiry of the 60-day toll-free transit window sits behind everything. Transmission: rising oil -> energy-CPI floors re-arm -> more CB hawkishness at the margin (the Fed's Sept tail, the BoE's dissents, the BoJ's inflation case, ECB energy language) - the exact reverse of last week's flow. CAD is the direct beneficiary (the glut was the whole bear case; both consensus CAD shorts are challenged), USD and JPY split the haven bid - JPY's amplified by the MoF intervention asymmetry at ~162 - while AUD and NZD wear the risk-off beta and GER40 loses its oil tailwind at record levels. The de-escalation scenario is equally live: if Doha convenes anyway and the strikes stay contained to anti-ship capability, the premium fades as fast as it built - which is why today's board carries no high-conviction trades and the oil-linked calls are sized small.
